volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 510 - Section 1.593)

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: σὺ δʼ οὐκ ἐπαιδεῖ , τῶνδε χωρὶς εἰ φρονεῖς ;
sections.translation: Are you not ashamed that your beliefs differ from theirs ?

sections.label: Section 511
sections.original: οὐδὲν γὰρ αἰσχρὸν τοὺς ὁμοσπλάγχνους σέβειν .
sections.translation: No , there is nothing shameful in respecting your own flesh and blood .

sections.label: Section 512
sections.original: οὔκουν ὅμαιμος χὠ καταντίον θανών ;
sections.translation: Was not he your brother too , who died in the opposite cause ?

sections.label: Section 513
sections.original: ὅμαιμος ἐκ μιᾶς τε καὶ ταὐτοῦ πατρός .
sections.translation: A brother by the same mother and the same father .

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original: πῶς δῆτʼ ἐκείνῳ δυσσεβῆ τιμᾷς χάριν ;
sections.translation: Why , then , do you pay a service that is disrespectful to him ?

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: οὐ μαρτυρήσει ταῦθʼ ὁ κατθανὼν νέκυς .
sections.translation: The dead man will not support you in that .

sections.label: Section 516
sections.original: εἴ τοί σφε τιμᾷς ἐξ ἴσου τῷ δυσσεβεῖ .
sections.translation: Yes , he will , if you honor him equally with the wicked one .

sections.label: Section 517
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι δοῦλος , ἀλλʼ ἀδελφὸς ὤλετο .
sections.translation: It was his brother , not his slave , who died .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: πορθῶν δὲ τήνδε γῆν · ὁ δʼ ἀντιστὰς ὕπερ .
sections.translation: But he died ravaging this land , while he fell in its defense .

sections.label: Section 519
sections.original: ὁμῶς ὅ γʼ Ἅιδης τοὺς νόμους τούτους ποθεῖ .
sections.translation: Hades craves these rites , nevertheless .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐχ ὁ χρηστὸς τῷ κακῷ λαχεῖν ἴσος .
sections.translation: But the good man craves a portion not equal to the evil ’ s .

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: τίς οἶδεν εἰ κάτωθεν εὐαγῆ τάδε ;
sections.translation: Who knows but that these actions are pure to those below ?

sections.label: Section 522
sections.original: οὔτοι ποθʼ οὑχθρός , οὐδʼ ὅταν θάνῃ , φίλος .
sections.translation: You do not love someone you have hated , not even after death .

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: οὔτοι συνέχθειν , ἀλλὰ συμφιλεῖν ἔφυν .
sections.translation: It is not my nature to join in hate , but in love .

sections.label: Section 524
sections.original: κάτω νυν ἐλθοῦσʼ , εἰ φιλητέον , φίλει
sections.translation: Then , go down to hell and love them

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: κείνους · ἐμοῦ δὲ ζῶντος οὐκ ἄρξει γυνή .
sections.translation: if you must . While I live , no woman will rule me .

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: καὶ μὴν πρὸ πυλῶν ἥδʼ Ἰσμήνη ,
sections.translation: Look , here comes Ismene from the palace , shedding the tears of a loving sister . A cloud over her eyes mars her red-flushed face ,

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: τέγγουσʼ εὐῶπα παρειάν .
sections.translation: and it breaks into rain on her comely cheek .

sections.label: Section 531
sections.original: σὺ δʼ , ἣ κατʼ οἴκους ὡς ἔχιδνʼ ὑφειμένη
sections.translation: You who were lurking like a viper in my own house and secretly gulping up my life ’ s blood , while I was oblivious that I was nurturing two plagues , two revolutions against my throne — tell me now , will you also affirm

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: φήσεις μετασχεῖν , ἢ ʼξομεῖ τὸ μὴ εἰδέναι ;
sections.translation: your share in this burial , or will you forswear all knowledge of it ?

sections.label: Section 536
sections.original: δέδρακα τοὔργον , εἴπερ ἥδʼ ὁμορροθεῖ
sections.translation: I performed the deed — as long as she concurs — and I share and carry the burden of guilt .

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἐάσει τοῦτό γʼ ἡ δίκη σʼ , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: No , justice will not permit you to do this , since you were not willing to help with the deed , nor did I give you a part in it .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐν κακοῖς τοῖς σοῖσιν οὐκ αἰσχύνομαι
sections.translation: But now with this sea of troubles around you , I am not ashamed to sail in a sea of suffering at your side .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.original: ὧν τοὔργον , Ἅιδης χοἰ κάτω ξυνίστορες ·
sections.translation: As to whose deed it is , Hades and the dead are witnesses . A friend in words is not the type of friend I love .

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: μήτοι , κασιγνήτη , μʼ ἀτιμάσῃς τὸ μὴ οὐ
sections.translation: No , sister , do not strip me of death ’ s honor ,

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: θανεῖν τε σὺν σοὶ τὸν θανόντα θʼ ἁγνίσαι .
sections.translation: but let me die with you and make due consecration to the dead .

sections.label: Section 546
sections.original: μή μοι θάνῃς σὺ κοινὰ μηδʼ ἃ μὴ ʼθιγες
sections.translation: Do not share my death . Do not claim deeds to which you did not put your hand . My death will suffice .

sections.label: Section 548
sections.original: καὶ τίς βίος μοι σοῦ λελειμμένῃ φίλος ;
sections.translation: And how can I cherish life , once I am deprived of you ?

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: Κρέοντʼ ἐρώτα · τοῦδε γὰρ σὺ κηδεμών .
sections.translation: Ask Creon . Your concern is for him .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: τί ταῦτʼ ἀνιᾷς μʼ , οὐδὲν ὠφελουμένη ;
sections.translation: Why do you torture me like this , when it does not help you ?

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: ἀλγοῦσα μὲν δῆτʼ εἰ γελῶ γʼ ἐν σοὶ γελῶ .
sections.translation: No , if I mock you , it is to my own pain that I do so .

sections.label: Section 552
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἂν ἀλλὰ νῦν σʼ ἔτʼ ὠφελοῖμʼ ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: Tell me , how can I help you , even now ?

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: σῶσον σεαυτήν · οὐ φθονῶ σʼ ὑπεκφυγεῖν .
sections.translation: Save yourself . I do not grudge your escape .

sections.label: Section 554
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα , κἀμπλάκω τοῦ σοῦ μόρου ;
sections.translation: Ah , misery ! Will I fall short of sharing your fate ?

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: σὺ μὲν γὰρ εἵλου ζῆν , ἐγὼ δὲ κατθανεῖν .
sections.translation: Your choice was to live , it was mine to die .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἐπʼ ἀρρήτοις γε τοῖς ἐμοῖς λόγοις .
sections.translation: At least your choice was not made without my protests .

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: καλῶς σὺ μὲν τοῖς , τοῖς δʼ ἐγὼ ʼδόκουν φρονεῖν .
sections.translation: One world approved your wisdom , another approved mine .

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἴση νῷν ἐστιν ἡ ʼξαμαρτία .
sections.translation: Nevertheless , the offense is identical for both of us .

sections.label: Section 559
sections.original: θάρσει · σὺ μὲν ζῇς , ἡ δʼ ἐμὴ ψυχὴ πάλαι
sections.translation: Take heart ! You live . But my life has long been

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: τέθνηκεν , ὥστε τοῖς θανοῦσιν ὠφελεῖν .
sections.translation: in Death ’ s hands so that I might serve the dead .

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: τὼ παῖδε φημὶ τώδε τὴν μὲν ἀρτίως
sections.translation: One of these maidens , I declare , has just revealed her foolishness ; the other has displayed it from the moment of her birth .

sections.label: Section 563
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ποτʼ , ὦναξ , οὐδʼ ὃς ἂν βλάστῃ μένει
sections.translation: Yes , Creon . Whatever amount of reason nature may have given them does not remain with those in dire straits , but goes astray .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: σοὶ γοῦν , ὅθʼ εἵλου σὺν κακοῖς πράσσειν κακά .
sections.translation: Yours did , I know , when you chose dire actions with dire allies .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: τί γὰρ μόνῃ μοι τῆσδʼ ἄτερ βιώσιμον ;
sections.translation: What life would there be for me alone , without her presence ?

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἥδε μέντοι μὴ λέγʼ · οὐ γὰρ ἔστʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: Do not speak of her presence . She lives no longer .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: ἀλλὰ κτενεῖς νυμφεῖα τοῦ σαυτοῦ τέκνου ;
sections.translation: What ? You will kill your own son ’ s bride ?

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: ἀρώσιμοι γὰρ χἀτέρων εἰσὶν γύαι .
sections.translation: Why not ? There are other fields for him to plough .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: οὐχ ὥς γʼ ἐκείνῳ τῇδέ τʼ ἦν ἡρμοσμένα .
sections.translation: But not fitted to him as she was .

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: κακὰς ἐγὼ γυναῖκας υἱέσι στυγῶ .
sections.translation: I abhor an evil wife for my son .

sections.label: Section 572
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθʼ Αἷμον , ὥς σʼ ἀτιμάζει πατήρ .
sections.translation: Haemon , dearest ! How your father wrongs you !

sections.label: Section 573
sections.original: ἄγαν γε λυπεῖς καὶ σὺ καὶ τὸ σὸν λέχος .
sections.translation: Enough ! Enough of you and of your marriage !

sections.label: Section 574
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ στερήσεις τῆσδε τὸν σαυτοῦ γόνον ;
sections.translation: Will you really cheat your son of this girl ?

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: Ἅιδης ὁ παύσων τούσδε τοὺς γάμους ἔφυ .
sections.translation: Death it is who will end these bridals for me .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: δεδογμένʼ , ὡς ἔοικε , τήνδε κατθανεῖν .
sections.translation: Then it seems that it is resolved that she will die .

sections.label: Section 577
sections.original: καὶ σοί γε κἀμοί . μὴ τριβὰς ἔτʼ , ἀλλά νιν
sections.translation: Resolved , yes , for you and by me . To the two Attendants . No more delay ! Servants , take them inside ! Hereafter they must be women , and not left at large .

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: φεύγουσι γάρ τοι χοἰ θρασεῖς , ὅταν πέλας
sections.translation: For it is known that even the brave seek to flee , when they see Death now closing on their life . Exeunt Attendants , guarding Antigone and Ismene . Creon remains .

sections.label: Section 1.583
sections.original: εὐδαίμονες οἷσι κακῶν ἄγευστος αἰών .
sections.translation: Blest are those whose days have not tasted of evil . For when a house has once been shaken by the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1.585
sections.original: οὐδὲν ἐλλείπει γενεᾶς ἐπὶ πλῆθος ἕρπον ·
sections.translation: no form of ruin is lacking , but it spreads over the bulk of the race , just as , when the surge is driven over the darkness of the deep by the fierce breath of Thracian sea-winds ,

sections.label: Section 1.590
sections.original: κυλίνδει βυσσόθεν κελαινὰν θῖνα καὶ
sections.translation: it rolls up the black sand from the depths , and the wind-beaten headlands that front the blows of the storm give out a mournful roar .

sections.label: Section 1.593
sections.original: ἀρχαῖα τὰ Λαβδακιδᾶν οἴκων ὁρῶμαι
sections.translation: I see that the ancient sorrows of the house of the Labdacids
